ab: Heza su haip pa zikang?
ch: M:你今天有空嘛?
en: Are you available today?

ab: Makuqa’ cunah?
ch: W:怎麼了?
en: What is the matter?

ab: Asa cak munsuaun tantungu.
ch: M:我想去拜訪你。
en: I would like to visit you.

ab: Heza nak haip pa sinkuzakuza. aza’qa moqnin.
ch: W:我今天有事不在家。改天吧。
en: I am out today. How about another day?

ab: Na heza su laqbing zikang? Maca tungqovalikin na kilimuku asu.
ch: M:那你明天有空嗎?我下午去找你。
en: Then, are you available tomorrow? I’ll visit you tomorrow afternoon.

ab: Maqatu, na iha’an cak lumaq mal’u su.
ch: W:可以，我在家等你。
en: Sure, I will wait for you at home.

ab: Maqatu cak makucia kikai siza isu tu qalinga? Na binuniun ku paitacan.
ch: M:請問我可以使用機器錄你的聲音嗎?我想做紀錄。
en: Would you mind if  I do the voice recording? I would like to keep a record.

ab: Uki! Na mopata i.
ch: W:好啊!沒有問題。
en: Sure! No problem.

ab: Maqatu siza qaningu su’u?
ch: M:可以錄影嗎?
en: Would you mind if I do the video recording?

ab: Ni cak manoaz sadu’an, ni amin na suku pinizavun, aza’qa nitu siza qaningu.
ch: W:我不好看，倉庫也沒整理，不要錄影啦。
en: No, I am plain and the warehouse hasn’t been tidied up yet.

ab: Na muaz cak lasasing.
ch: M:那我拍照就好。
en: Then I will just take photos.

ab: Ni amin maqtu lasasing.
ch: W:拍照也不要。
en: No photographing either.

ab: Mikomicang maqtu’an cak muckun su palakancipa.
ch: M:謝謝你讓我訪問。
en: Thank you for accepting the interview.

ab: Nitu makuaq, maqa su a asaun masingav dau?
ch: W:沒問題，你想問什麼?
en: You are welcome. What would you like to know?

ab: Malqamaq’ asu tu talmindu ka sinkuzakuza?
ch: M:請問您年輕時是做什麼的?
en: What do you do when you are young?

ab: Kisacu’ cak qabas’can qaitu mal’ukin cak lopaku.
ch: W:我以前是警察，但我現在退休了。
en: I was a police officer but I am retired now.

ab: Oqa haza su lopaku sinkuazakuza?
ch: M:那你現在有工作嗎?
en: Then, are you working at the moment?

ab: Ha’an cak kakuta kuzakuza.
ch: W:我在學校上班。
en: I am working at school.

ab: Ha’an amin su’u a uva’az acang miqomic?
ch: M:請問您的孩子也住在部落生活嗎?
en: Do your kids live in the tribe too?

ab: Ni, ha’an nak ka uvava’az natata miqomic.
ch: W:不，我的孩子在都市居住。
en: No, my kids are all living in the city.

ab: Na tatini qana asu ha’an lumaq ki?
ch: M:那你自己一個人住嗎?
en: Then, do you live by yourself?

ab: Heza cak sepuk tac’a acu cin loqe, inak amin ngka tactulumqa.
ch: W:我還有養一隻狗和一隻鳥，他們也是我的家人。
en: I also keep a dog and a bird. They are my family.

ab: Heza amin cak sepuk tac’a ngaung, nitu macmuav qalimulmul maca heza a sinsepuk.
ch: M:我也有養一隻貓，有動物陪伴不會孤單寂寞。
en: I have a cat too. I won’t get bored with the company of my pet.

ab: Asa cak macingav su paliuni ki qanup tu samu.
ch: M:我想請問你關於打獵的禁忌。
en: I would like to ask you about the taboos of hunting.

ab: Ni nak madeia a qancepun, qaitu maca qancepuk ku ka na aminun ku taqo su.
ch: W:我知道的不多，但我所知道的都會回答你。
en: I don’t know much about that, but I will tell you everything I know.

ab: Maqa mu a isqanup tu busul la, heza maqa’ tu samu?
ch: M:你們打獵的獵槍，有什麼禁忌嗎?
en: Do you have any taboos about hunting weapons?

ab: Ma nam ma busul la ni a binanoaz maqatu katidu.
ch: W:我們打獵的獵槍不可以讓女生碰到。
en: Our hunting weapons are untouchable to women.

ab: Na maqatu a binanoaz lavi qanup?
ch: M:那女人可以一起去打獵嗎?
en: Then, can women go hunting together?

ab: Nitumaqtu, ni amin na binanoaz maqatu hamu qanup.
ch: W:不行，女人也不可以打獵。
en: No, women cannot go hunting either.
